2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026）will be held during June 26-28, 2026.
The following information about the schedule is for your reference:
Day 1- Jun. 26, 2026
13:00-17:00 Registration
Day 2- Jun. 27, 2026
09:00-12:00 Keynote Speakers12:00-14:00 Lunch14:00-17:00 Oral Speeches18:00-19:30 Banquet
Day 3- Jun. 28, 2026
09:00-18:00 Academic tour
* The above program is a brief outline. A more detailed programme will be emailed to you after the registration deadline. The actual conference schedule may be a little different according to the number of participants.
Instruction for Oral Presentations
1. Timing: A maximum of 15 minutes in total, including presentation and discussion. Please be considerable once the Program is completed the next speaker should also be allocated with reasonable amount of time.
2. For conference presentation, you can use your own computer or copy your presentation file into the conference computer from CD or USB drive, and make sure you have scanned and cleaned viruses beforehand. Speakers are required to meet her/his session Chair in the corresponding session room at least 10 minutes before the session starts to get things ready or copy the slide file (PPT or PDF) into the conference computer.
3. It is also suggested that you email a copy of your presentation to your personal email box as a backup. If for some reason the files cannot be accessed from your flash drive, you can download them to the computer from your email.
4. Please note that each session room will be equipped with an LCD projector, screen, laser pointer, microphone, and a laptop with general presentation software such as the Microsoft PowerPoint and Adobe Reader. Please make sure that your files are compatible and readable with our operation system by using commonly used fronts and symbols. If you plan to use your own computer, please test the connection and make sure it works well before your presentation.
5. Movies: If your files contain movies please make sure that they are well formatted and connected to the main files.
Instructions for Poster Presentations
1. Maximum poster size is 59.4 CM wide by 84.1 CM high (A1).
2. Posters are required to be optimized and attractive. Information should be visible from a proper distance, say, one meter away.
3. Please note that during your poster session, you as the author should stay by your poster and ready to explain and discuss your paper with visiting delegates.

Welcome to ICAIEM 2026
The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management
The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026) will be held during June 26-28, 2026. The conference aims to bring together researchers, industry leaders and technical experts from around the world to discuss the latest applications, challenges and future trends of artificial intelligence in engineering management. In the rapidly changing business environment, the intelligence of project management has become a key factor to enhance the competitiveness of enterprises. With its powerful data analysis ability and decision support system, artificial intelligence technology is profoundly changing the operation mode of traditional management. In terms of project planning, schedule control and resource allocation, the application of AI is driving the industry to a new stage of greater efficiency and flexibility. The conference will invite renowned scholars and business representatives from around the world to share their latest research results and practical experience in the intersection of artificial intelligence and engineering management. ICAIEM 2026 looks forward to your participation in moving towards a better future together.
We warmly invite you to participate in ICAIEM 2026 and look forward to seeing you!

Details of The Attending Registration
Join as Presenter
If you are only interested in giving a presentation at the conference without publishing your paper in the proceedings, you can choose to attend ICAIEM 2026 as a Presenter. As a presenter, you need to submit the Abstract and Title of your presentation during the registration. For further information, please contact us at: icaiem@126.com
Join as Listener
ICAIEM 2026 is an unmissable conference. It is a good chance and an effective platform for you to meet many renowned experts and researchers in the field of the latest academic research. You are welcome to attend this great event. You just need to complete the registration as a Listener before the registration deadline.
Details of The Registration Fees
1. Registration fees for ICAIEM2026 Regular is 540 USD / 3600 CNY per paper.
2. If the length of the paper exceeds 8 Pages, the cost of Each Extra Page (begin at Page 9) is 55 USD / 400 CNY.
3. Registration fees include conference proceedings, lunches, souvenirs and attending all technical sessions.
4. The registration fee does not include:
• Accommodation
• Visa application fee
• Transportation fare
5. At least one author for each accepted final paper must pre-register.
6. Completed registrations will be acknowledged by the Organizing Committee within 2-5 workdays after receiving your payment.
7.And at least 30% of the registration fee will be charged as the service fee if you retract your paper for personal reasons.
8. Important note to authors about the new ACM open access publishing model, all papers in the ICAIEM2026 proceedings will be published open access (OA) on the ACM Digital Library. For authors who are not affiliated with member institutions of the ACM Open Program, ACM charges an article processing charge (APC) for OA publication. However, the ICAIEM 2026 conference committee has agreed that the conference will pay all applicable APCs. All papers will therefore be published OA at no cost to authors.

Link: History
Title: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025)
The event was successfully held at
Guangzhou College of Technology and Business.
June 28th, 2025，2025
International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025）it was grandly held at the International Education Exchange Center of Guangzhou University of Commerce in San Shui District, Foshan.
This conference is jointly organized by Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and Guangdong Ais Information Academic Exchange Research Institute. It is supported by Guangzhou Dongruan University, Guangzhou Business College, Guangzhou City Polytechnic, Guangzhou Applied Science University, Guangzhou Xinhua University, Guangzhou Institute of Technology, and South China Agricultural University - Zhujiang College. It is jointly hosted by the School of
Management of Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Smart Cold Chain Industry College.
The conference officially opened on the morning of June 28th at the Sansui Campus of Guangzhou Institute of Technology. The meeting was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from Guangzhou Institute of Technology. Representatives of the organizing committee warmly welcomed the presence of scholars from all walks of life. The conference was held in a combination of online and offline formats, gathering over 150 experts and scholars from around the world, including institutions such as Jinan University, Guangdong University of Technology, University of Sydney, Yunnan University, Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Liaoning Institute of Technology, Armed Police Engineering University, Southwest Forestry University, Jimei University, Guangdong Peizheng College, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., Zhuhai Science and Technology College Big Data College, and China Automobile Data Co., Ltd. The participants discussed in depth the latest research achievements and future development trends in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
Qianyuan Wang, the vice president of Guangzhou University of Commerce, delivered the opening speech on behalf of the organizers. He expressed his sincere gratitude to the domestic and foreign experts and scholars, as well as the sister universities and industry organizations, who have long been concerned and supported the development of the school. The conference focused on the hot and difficult issues in the field, promoting interdisciplinary research and the integration of industry-academia-research. It invited experts from multiple universities to have in-depth exchanges and jointly promote academic development and technological innovation. Vice President Wang hopes that all experts and scholars will offer valuable suggestions and work together to promote the innovative development of the engineering management discipline and contribute to the progress of the industry.
The meeting agenda consists of main venue reports, invited reports, and oral presentations. This main venue focuses on sharing on key topics such as "precision manufacturing around the value chain, post-5G industrial Internet of Things, digital consumer value co-creation, and green supply chain and corporate sustainable development". Professor Li Congdong from Jinan University, Professor Du Songhua from Guangdong University of Technology, Professor Yonghui Li from the University of Sydney in Australia, and Professor Zhiping Hou from Guangzhou University of Commerce respectively delivered keynote speeches. Experts and scholars presented their latest research results and academic insights in related fields, which aroused extensive attention and lively discussions among the attending experts and scholars. The academic atmosphere in the venue was very intense.
On the afternoon of the 28th, the afternoon session of the conference was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from the School of Management of Guangzhou Gongshang University. Associate Professor Hu Zuwu from Guangzhou Business College, Dr. Liu Chao from Guangzhou Gongshang University, Dr. Yujie Shi, and Dr.Yaqing Liang respectively delivered invited presentations. Subsequently, five experts from Guangzhou Business College, Jimei University, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., and Beijing Jiaotong University gave oral reports. The conference topics covered multiple cutting-edge development directions such as project risk assessment based on deep learning and large language models, image forgery detection, and the impact of supply chain digital transformation performance. It provided rich opportunities for academic exchanges and cooperation for the participants, promoting the continuous development and innovation of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
This event facilitated the exchange of scientific information among researchers, developers, engineers, students and practitioners in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management from all over the world. Through discussions and sharing, the practical experiences, thoughts and the most cutting-edge research achievements in related fields of scholars from around the globe were widely disseminated, which holds positive significance for addressing new challenges in the future.

Instruction for Oral Presentations
1. Timing: A maximum of 15 minutes in total, including presentation and discussion. Please be considerable once the Program is completed the next speaker should also be allocated with reasonable amount of time.
2. For conference presentation, you can use your own computer or copy your presentation file into the conference computer from CD or USB drive, and make sure you have scanned and cleaned viruses beforehand. Speakers are required to meet her/his session Chair in the corresponding session room at least 10 minutes before the session starts to get things ready or copy the slide file (PPT or PDF) into the conference computer.
3. It is also suggested that you email a copy of your presentation to your personal email box as a backup. If for some reason the files cannot be accessed from your flash drive, you can download them to the computer from your email.
4. Please note that each session room will be equipped with an LCD projector, screen, laser pointer, microphone, and a laptop with general presentation software such as the Microsoft PowerPoint and Adobe Reader. Please make sure that your files are compatible and readable with our operation system by using commonly used fronts and symbols. If you plan to use your own computer, please test the connection and make sure it works well before your presentation.
5. Movies: If your files contain movies please make sure that they are well formatted and connected to the main files.
Instructions for Poster Presentations
1. Maximum poster size is 59.4 CM wide by 84.1 CM high (A1).
2. Posters are required to be optimized and attractive. Information should be visible from a proper distance, say, one meter away.

Title: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025)
The event was successfully held at
Guangzhou College of Technology and Business.
June 28th, 2025，2025
International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025）it was grandly held at the International Education Exchange Center of Guangzhou University of Commerce in San Shui District, Foshan.
This conference is jointly organized by Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and Guangdong Ais Information Academic Exchange Research Institute. It is supported by Guangzhou Dongruan University, Guangzhou Business College, Guangzhou City Polytechnic, Guangzhou Applied Science University, Guangzhou Xinhua University, Guangzhou Institute of Technology, and South China Agricultural University - Zhujiang College. It is jointly hosted by the School of
Management of Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Smart Cold Chain Industry College.
The conference officially opened on the morning of June 28th at the Sansui Campus of Guangzhou Institute of Technology. The meeting was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from Guangzhou Institute of Technology. Representatives of the organizing committee warmly welcomed the presence of scholars from all walks of life. The conference was held in a combination of online and offline formats, gathering over 150 experts and scholars from around the world, including institutions such as Jinan University, Guangdong University of Technology, University of Sydney, Yunnan University, Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Liaoning Institute of Technology, Armed Police Engineering University, Southwest Forestry University, Jimei University, Guangdong Peizheng College, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., Zhuhai Science and Technology College Big Data College, and China Automobile Data Co., Ltd. The participants discussed in depth the latest research achievements and future development trends in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
Qianyuan Wang, the vice president of Guangzhou University of Commerce, delivered the opening speech on behalf of the organizers. He expressed his sincere gratitude to the domestic and foreign experts and scholars, as well as the sister universities and industry organizations, who have long been concerned and supported the development of the school. The conference focused on the hot and difficult issues in the field, promoting interdisciplinary research and the integration of industry-academia-research. It invited experts from multiple universities to have in-depth exchanges and jointly promote academic development and technological innovation. Vice President Wang hopes that all experts and scholars will offer valuable suggestions and work together to promote the innovative development of the engineering management discipline and contribute to the progress of the industry.
The meeting agenda consists of main venue reports, invited reports, and oral presentations. This main venue focuses on sharing on key topics such as "precision manufacturing around the value chain, post-5G industrial Internet of Things, digital consumer value co-creation, and green supply chain and corporate sustainable development". Professor Li Congdong from Jinan University, Professor Du Songhua from Guangdong University of Technology, Professor Yonghui Li from the University of Sydney in Australia, and Professor Zhiping Hou from Guangzhou University of Commerce respectively delivered keynote speeches. Experts and scholars presented their latest research results and academic insights in related fields, which aroused extensive attention and lively discussions among the attending experts and scholars. The academic atmosphere in the venue was very intense.
On the afternoon of the 28th, the afternoon session of the conference was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from the School of Management of Guangzhou Gongshang University. Associate Professor Hu Zuwu from Guangzhou Business College, Dr. Liu Chao from Guangzhou Gongshang University, Dr. Yujie Shi, and Dr.Yaqing Liang respectively delivered invited presentations. Subsequently, five experts from Guangzhou Business College, Jimei University, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., and Beijing Jiaotong University gave oral reports. The conference topics covered multiple cutting-edge development directions such as project risk assessment based on deep learning and large language models, image forgery detection, and the impact of supply chain digital transformation performance. It provided rich opportunities for academic exchanges and cooperation for the participants, promoting the continuous development and innovation of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
This event facilitated the exchange of scientific information among researchers, developers, engineers, students and practitioners in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management from all over the world. Through discussions and sharing, the practical experiences, thoughts and the most cutting-edge research achievements in related fields of scholars from around the globe were widely disseminated, which holds positive significance for addressing new challenges in the future.

The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026) will be held during June 26-28, 2026. The conference aims to bring together researchers, industry leaders and technical experts from around the world to discuss the latest applications, challenges and future trends of artificial intelligence in engineering management. In the rapidly changing business environment, the intelligence of project management has become a key factor to enhance the competitiveness of enterprises. With its powerful data analysis ability and decision support system, artificial intelligence technology is profoundly changing the operation mode of traditional management. In terms of project planning, schedule control and resource allocation, the application of AI is driving the industry to a new stage of greater efficiency and flexibility. The conference will invite renowned scholars and business representatives from around the world to share their latest research results and practical experience in the intersection of artificial intelligence and engineering management. ICAIEM 2026 looks forward to your participation in moving towards a better future together.

Link: History
Title: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025)
The event was successfully held at
Guangzhou College of Technology and Business.
June 28th, 2025，2025
International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025）it was grandly held at the International Education Exchange Center of Guangzhou University of Commerce in San Shui District, Foshan.
This conference is jointly organized by Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and Guangdong Ais Information Academic Exchange Research Institute. It is supported by Guangzhou Dongruan University, Guangzhou Business College, Guangzhou City Polytechnic, Guangzhou Applied Science University, Guangzhou Xinhua University, Guangzhou Institute of Technology, and South China Agricultural University - Zhujiang College. It is jointly hosted by the School of
Management of Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Smart Cold Chain Industry College.
The conference officially opened on the morning of June 28th at the Sansui Campus of Guangzhou Institute of Technology. The meeting was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from Guangzhou Institute of Technology. Representatives of the organizing committee warmly welcomed the presence of scholars from all walks of life. The conference was held in a combination of online and offline formats, gathering over 150 experts and scholars from around the world, including institutions such as Jinan University, Guangdong University of Technology, University of Sydney, Yunnan University, Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Liaoning Institute of Technology, Armed Police Engineering University, Southwest Forestry University, Jimei University, Guangdong Peizheng College, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., Zhuhai Science and Technology College Big Data College, and China Automobile Data Co., Ltd. The participants discussed in depth the latest research achievements and future development trends in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
Qianyuan Wang, the vice president of Guangzhou University of Commerce, delivered the opening speech on behalf of the organizers. He expressed his sincere gratitude to the domestic and foreign experts and scholars, as well as the sister universities and industry organizations, who have long been concerned and supported the development of the school. The conference focused on the hot and difficult issues in the field, promoting interdisciplinary research and the integration of industry-academia-research. It invited experts from multiple universities to have in-depth exchanges and jointly promote academic development and technological innovation. Vice President Wang hopes that all experts and scholars will offer valuable suggestions and work together to promote the innovative development of the engineering management discipline and contribute to the progress of the industry.
The meeting agenda consists of main venue reports, invited reports, and oral presentations. This main venue focuses on sharing on key topics such as "precision manufacturing around the value chain, post-5G industrial Internet of Things, digital consumer value co-creation, and green supply chain and corporate sustainable development". Professor Li Congdong from Jinan University, Professor Du Songhua from Guangdong University of Technology, Professor Yonghui Li from the University of Sydney in Australia, and Professor Zhiping Hou from Guangzhou University of Commerce respectively delivered keynote speeches. Experts and scholars presented their latest research results and academic insights in related fields, which aroused extensive attention and lively discussions among the attending experts and scholars. The academic atmosphere in the venue was very intense.
On the afternoon of the 28th, the afternoon session of the conference was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from the School of Management of Guangzhou Gongshang University. Associate Professor Hu Zuwu from Guangzhou Business College, Dr. Liu Chao from Guangzhou Gongshang University, Dr. Yujie Shi, and Dr.Yaqing Liang respectively delivered invited presentations. Subsequently, five experts from Guangzhou Business College, Jimei University, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., and Beijing Jiaotong University gave oral reports. The conference topics covered multiple cutting-edge development directions such as project risk assessment based on deep learning and large language models, image forgery detection, and the impact of supply chain digital transformation performance. It provided rich opportunities for academic exchanges and cooperation for the participants, promoting the continuous development and innovation of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
This event facilitated the exchange of scientific information among researchers, developers, engineers, students and practitioners in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management from all over the world. Through discussions and sharing, the practical experiences, thoughts and the most cutting-edge research achievements in related fields of scholars from around the globe were widely disseminated, which holds positive significance for addressing new challenges in the future.

The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026）will be held during June 26-28, 2026.

* The above program is a brief outline. A more detailed programme will be emailed to you after the registration deadline. The actual conference schedule may be a little different according to the number of participants.
Instruction for Oral Presentations
1. Timing: A maximum of 15 minutes in total, including presentation and discussion. Please be considerable once the Program is completed the next speaker should also be allocated with reasonable amount of time.
2. For conference presentation, you can use your own computer or copy your presentation file into the conference computer from CD or USB drive, and make sure you have scanned and cleaned viruses beforehand. Speakers are required to meet her/his session Chair in the corresponding session room at least 10 minutes before the session starts to get things ready or copy the slide file (PPT or PDF) into the conference computer.
3. It is also suggested that you email a copy of your presentation to your personal email box as a backup. If for some reason the files cannot be accessed from your flash drive, you can download them to the computer from your email.
4. Please note that each session room will be equipped with an LCD projector, screen, laser pointer, microphone, and a laptop with general presentation software such as the Microsoft PowerPoint and Adobe Reader. Please make sure that your files are compatible and readable with our operation system by using commonly used fronts and symbols. If you plan to use your own computer, please test the connection and make sure it works well before your presentation.
5. Movies: If your files contain movies please make sure that they are well formatted and connected to the main files.
Instructions for Poster Presentations
1. Maximum poster size is 59.4 CM wide by 84.1 CM high (A1).
2. Posters are required to be optimized and attractive. Information should be visible from a proper distance, say, one meter away.
3. Please note that during your poster session, you as the author should stay by your poster and ready to explain and discuss your paper with visiting delegates.

• Accommodation
• Visa application fee
• Transportation fare
5. At least one author for each accepted final paper must pre-register.
6. Completed registrations will be acknowledged by the Organizing Committee within 2-5 workdays after receiving your payment.
7.And at least 30% of the registration fee will be charged as the service fee if you retract your paper for personal reasons.
8. Important note to authors about the new ACM open access publishing model, all papers in the ICAIEM2026 proceedings will be published open access (OA) on the ACM Digital Library. However, the ICAIEM 2026 conference committee has agreed that the conference will pay all applicable APCs. All papers will therefore be published OA at no cost to authors.

2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025)
The event was successfully held at
Guangzhou College of Technology and Business.
June 28th, 2025，2025
International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025）it was grandly held at the International Education Exchange Center of Guangzhou University of Commerce in San Shui District, Foshan.
This conference is jointly organized by Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and Guangdong Ais Information Academic Exchange Research Institute. It is supported by Guangzhou Dongruan University, Guangzhou Business College, Guangzhou City Polytechnic, Guangzhou Applied Science University, Guangzhou Xinhua University, Guangzhou Institute of Technology, and South China Agricultural University - Zhujiang College. It is jointly hosted by the School of
Management of Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Smart Cold Chain Industry College.
The conference officially opened on the morning of June 28th at the Sansui Campus of Guangzhou Institute of Technology. The meeting was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from Guangzhou Institute of Technology. Representatives of the organizing committee warmly welcomed the presence of scholars from all walks of life. The conference was held in a combination of online and offline formats, gathering over 150 experts and scholars from around the world, including institutions such as Jinan University, Guangdong University of Technology, University of Sydney, Yunnan University, Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Liaoning Institute of Technology, Armed Police Engineering University, Southwest Forestry University, Jimei University, Guangdong Peizheng College, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., Zhuhai Science and Technology College Big Data College, and China Automobile Data Co., Ltd. The participants discussed in depth the latest research achievements and future development trends in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
Qianyuan Wang, the vice president of Guangzhou University of Commerce, delivered the opening speech on behalf of the organizers. He expressed his sincere gratitude to the domestic and foreign experts and scholars, as well as the sister universities and industry organizations, who have long been concerned and supported the development of the school. The conference focused on the hot and difficult issues in the field, promoting interdisciplinary research and the integration of industry-academia-research. It invited experts from multiple universities to have in-depth exchanges and jointly promote academic development and technological innovation. Vice President Wang hopes that all experts and scholars will offer valuable suggestions and work together to promote the innovative development of the engineering management discipline and contribute to the progress of the industry.
The meeting agenda consists of main venue reports, invited reports, and oral presentations. This main venue focuses on sharing on key topics such as "precision manufacturing around the value chain, post-5G industrial Internet of Things, digital consumer value co-creation, and green supply chain and corporate sustainable development". Professor Li Congdong from Jinan University, Professor Du Songhua from Guangdong University of Technology, Professor Yonghui Li from the University of Sydney in Australia, and Professor Zhiping Hou from Guangzhou University of Commerce respectively delivered keynote speeches. Experts and scholars presented their latest research results and academic insights in related fields, which aroused extensive attention and lively discussions among the attending experts and scholars. The academic atmosphere in the venue was very intense.
On the afternoon of the 28th, the afternoon session of the conference was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from the School of Management of Guangzhou Gongshang University. Associate Professor Hu Zuwu from Guangzhou Business College, Dr. Liu Chao from Guangzhou Gongshang University, Dr. Yujie Shi, and Dr.Yaqing Liang respectively delivered invited presentations. Subsequently, five experts from Guangzhou Business College, Jimei University, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., and Beijing Jiaotong University gave oral reports. The conference topics covered multiple cutting-edge development directions such as project risk assessment based on deep learning and large language models, image forgery detection, and the impact of supply chain digital transformation performance. It provided rich opportunities for academic exchanges and cooperation for the participants, promoting the continuous development and innovation of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
This event facilitated the exchange of scientific information among researchers, developers, engineers, students and practitioners in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management from all over the world. Through discussions and sharing, the practical experiences, thoughts and the most cutting-edge research achievements in related fields of scholars from around the globe were widely disseminated, which holds positive significance for addressing new challenges in the future.

Welcome to ICAIEM 2026
The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management
The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026) will be held during June 26-28, 2026. The conference aims to bring together researchers, industry leaders and technical experts from around the world to discuss the latest applications, challenges and future trends of artificial intelligence in engineering management. In the rapidly changing business environment, the intelligence of project management has become a key factor to enhance the competitiveness of enterprises. With its powerful data analysis ability and decision support system, artificial intelligence technology is profoundly changing the operation mode of traditional management. In terms of project planning, schedule control and resource allocation, the application of AI is driving the industry to a new stage of greater efficiency and flexibility. The conference will invite renowned scholars and business representatives from around the world to share their latest research results and practical experience in the intersection of artificial intelligence and engineering management. ICAIEM 2026 looks forward to your participation in moving towards a better future together.
We warmly invite you to participate in ICAIEM 2026 and look forward to seeing you!

Link: History
Title: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025)
The event was successfully held at
Guangzhou College of Technology and Business.
June 28th, 2025，2025
International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025）it was grandly held at the International Education Exchange Center of Guangzhou University of Commerce in San Shui District, Foshan.
This conference is jointly organized by Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and Guangdong Ais Information Academic Exchange Research Institute. It is supported by Guangzhou Dongruan University, Guangzhou Business College, Guangzhou City Polytechnic, Guangzhou Applied Science University, Guangzhou Xinhua University, Guangzhou Institute of Technology, and South China Agricultural University - Zhujiang College. It is jointly hosted by the School of
Management of Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Smart Cold Chain Industry College.
The conference officially opened on the morning of June 28th at the Sansui Campus of Guangzhou Institute of Technology. The meeting was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from Guangzhou Institute of Technology. Representatives of the organizing committee warmly welcomed the presence of scholars from all walks of life. The conference was held in a combination of online and offline formats, gathering over 150 experts and scholars from around the world, including institutions such as Jinan University, Guangdong University of Technology, University of Sydney, Yunnan University, Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Liaoning Institute of Technology, Armed Police Engineering University, Southwest Forestry University, Jimei University, Guangdong Peizheng College, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., Zhuhai Science and Technology College Big Data College, and China Automobile Data Co., Ltd. The participants discussed in depth the latest research achievements and future development trends in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
Qianyuan Wang, the vice president of Guangzhou University of Commerce, delivered the opening speech on behalf of the organizers. He expressed his sincere gratitude to the domestic and foreign experts and scholars, as well as the sister universities and industry organizations, who have long been concerned and supported the development of the school. The conference focused on the hot and difficult issues in the field, promoting interdisciplinary research and the integration of industry-academia-research. It invited experts from multiple universities to have in-depth exchanges and jointly promote academic development and technological innovation. Vice President Wang hopes that all experts and scholars will offer valuable suggestions and work together to promote the innovative development of the engineering management discipline and contribute to the progress of the industry.
The meeting agenda consists of main venue reports, invited reports, and oral presentations. This main venue focuses on sharing on key topics such as "precision manufacturing around the value chain, post-5G industrial Internet of Things, digital consumer value co-creation, and green supply chain and corporate sustainable development". Professor Li Congdong from Jinan University, Professor Du Songhua from Guangdong University of Technology, Professor Yonghui Li from the University of Sydney in Australia, and Professor Zhiping Hou from Guangzhou University of Commerce respectively delivered keynote speeches. Experts and scholars presented their latest research results and academic insights in related fields, which aroused extensive attention and lively discussions among the attending experts and scholars. The academic atmosphere in the venue was very intense.
On the afternoon of the 28th, the afternoon session of the conference was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from the School of Management of Guangzhou Gongshang University. Associate Professor Hu Zuwu from Guangzhou Business College, Dr. Liu Chao from Guangzhou Gongshang University, Dr. Yujie Shi, and Dr.Yaqing Liang respectively delivered invited presentations. Subsequently, five experts from Guangzhou Business College, Jimei University, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., and Beijing Jiaotong University gave oral reports. The conference topics covered multiple cutting-edge development directions such as project risk assessment based on deep learning and large language models, image forgery detection, and the impact of supply chain digital transformation performance. It provided rich opportunities for academic exchanges and cooperation for the participants, promoting the continuous development and innovation of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
This event facilitated the exchange of scientific information among researchers, developers, engineers, students and practitioners in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management from all over the world. Through discussions and sharing, the practical experiences, thoughts and the most cutting-edge research achievements in related fields of scholars from around the globe were widely disseminated, which holds positive significance for addressing new challenges in the future.

Welcome to ICAIEM 2026
The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management
The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026) will be held during June 26-28, 2026. The conference aims to bring together researchers, industry leaders and technical experts from around the world to discuss the latest applications, challenges and future trends of artificial intelligence in engineering management. In the rapidly changing business environment, the intelligence of project management has become a key factor to enhance the competitiveness of enterprises. With its powerful data analysis ability and decision support system, artificial intelligence technology is profoundly changing the operation mode of traditional management. In terms of project planning, schedule control and resource allocation, the application of AI is driving the industry to a new stage of greater efficiency and flexibility. The conference will invite renowned scholars and business representatives from around the world to share their latest research results and practical experience in the intersection of artificial intelligence and engineering management. ICAIEM 2026 looks forward to your participation in moving towards a better future together.
We warmly invite you to participate in ICAIEM 2026 and look forward to seeing you!

Title: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025)
The event was successfully held at
Guangzhou College of Technology and Business.
June 28th, 2025，2025
International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025）it was grandly held at the International Education Exchange Center of Guangzhou University of Commerce in San Shui District, Foshan.
This conference is jointly organized by Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and Guangdong Ais Information Academic Exchange Research Institute. It is supported by Guangzhou Dongruan University, Guangzhou Business College, Guangzhou City Polytechnic, Guangzhou Applied Science University, Guangzhou Xinhua University, Guangzhou Institute of Technology, and South China Agricultural University - Zhujiang College. It is jointly hosted by the School of
Management of Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Smart Cold Chain Industry College.
The conference officially opened on the morning of June 28th at the Sansui Campus of Guangzhou Institute of Technology. The meeting was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from Guangzhou Institute of Technology. Representatives of the organizing committee warmly welcomed the presence of scholars from all walks of life. The conference was held in a combination of online and offline formats, gathering over 150 experts and scholars from around the world, including institutions such as Jinan University, Guangdong University of Technology, University of Sydney, Yunnan University, Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Liaoning Institute of Technology, Armed Police Engineering University, Southwest Forestry University, Jimei University, Guangdong Peizheng College, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., Zhuhai Science and Technology College Big Data College, and China Automobile Data Co., Ltd. The participants discussed in depth the latest research achievements and future development trends in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
Qianyuan Wang, the vice president of Guangzhou University of Commerce, delivered the opening speech on behalf of the organizers. He expressed his sincere gratitude to the domestic and foreign experts and scholars, as well as the sister universities and industry organizations, who have long been concerned and supported the development of the school. The conference focused on the hot and difficult issues in the field, promoting interdisciplinary research and the integration of industry-academia-research. It invited experts from multiple universities to have in-depth exchanges and jointly promote academic development and technological innovation. Vice President Wang hopes that all experts and scholars will offer valuable suggestions and work together to promote the innovative development of the engineering management discipline and contribute to the progress of the industry.
The meeting agenda consists of main venue reports, invited reports, and oral presentations. This main venue focuses on sharing on key topics such as "precision manufacturing around the value chain, post-5G industrial Internet of Things, digital consumer value co-creation, and green supply chain and corporate sustainable development". Professor Li Congdong from Jinan University, Professor Du Songhua from Guangdong University of Technology, Professor Yonghui Li from the University of Sydney in Australia, and Professor Zhiping Hou from Guangzhou University of Commerce respectively delivered keynote speeches. Experts and scholars presented their latest research results and academic insights in related fields, which aroused extensive attention and lively discussions among the attending experts and scholars. The academic atmosphere in the venue was very intense.
On the afternoon of the 28th, the afternoon session of the conference was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from the School of Management of Guangzhou Gongshang University. Associate Professor Hu Zuwu from Guangzhou Business College, Dr. Liu Chao from Guangzhou Gongshang University, Dr. Yujie Shi, and Dr.Yaqing Liang respectively delivered invited presentations. Subsequently, five experts from Guangzhou Business College, Jimei University, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., and Beijing Jiaotong University gave oral reports. The conference topics covered multiple cutting-edge development directions such as project risk assessment based on deep learning and large language models, image forgery detection, and the impact of supply chain digital transformation performance. It provided rich opportunities for academic exchanges and cooperation for the participants, promoting the continuous development and innovation of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
This event facilitated the exchange of scientific information among researchers, developers, engineers, students and practitioners in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management from all over the world. Through discussions and sharing, the practical experiences, thoughts and the most cutting-edge research achievements in related fields of scholars from around the globe were widely disseminated, which holds positive significance for addressing new challenges in the future.

2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
The 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026）will be held during June 26-28, 2026.
The following information about the schedule is for your reference:
Day 1- Jun. 26, 2026
13:00-17:00 Registration
Day 2- Jun. 27, 2026
09:00-12:00 Keynote Speakers12:00-14:00 Lunch14:00-17:00 Oral Speeches18:00-19:30 Banquet
Day 3- Jun. 28, 2026
09:00-18:00 Academic tour
* The above program is a brief outline. A more detailed programme will be emailed to you after the registration deadline. The actual conference schedule may be a little different according to the number of participants.
Instruction for Oral Presentations
1. Timing: A maximum of 15 minutes in total, including presentation and discussion. Please be considerable once the Program is completed the next speaker should also be allocated with reasonable amount of time.
2. For conference presentation, you can use your own computer or copy your presentation file into the conference computer from CD or USB drive, and make sure you have scanned and cleaned viruses beforehand. Speakers are required to meet her/his session Chair in the corresponding session room at least 10 minutes before the session starts to get things ready or copy the slide file (PPT or PDF) into the conference computer.
3. It is also suggested that you email a copy of your presentation to your personal email box as a backup. If for some reason the files cannot be accessed from your flash drive, you can download them to the computer from your email.
4. Please note that each session room will be equipped with an LCD projector, screen, laser pointer, microphone, and a laptop with general presentation software such as the Microsoft PowerPoint and Adobe Reader. Please make sure that your files are compatible and readable with our operation system by using commonly used fronts and symbols. If you plan to use your own computer, please test the connection and make sure it works well before your presentation.
5. Movies: If your files contain movies please make sure that they are well formatted and connected to the main files.
Instructions for Poster Presentations
1. Maximum poster size is 59.4 CM wide by 84.1 CM high (A1).
2. Posters are required to be optimized and attractive. Information should be visible from a proper distance, say, one meter away.
3. Please note that during your poster session, you as the author should stay by your poster and ready to explain and discuss your paper with visiting delegates.

In terms of project planning, schedule control and resource allocation, the application of AI is driving the industry to a new stage of greater efficiency and flexibility. The conference will invite renowned scholars and business representatives from around the world to share their latest research results and practical experience in the intersection of artificial intelligence and engineering management. ICAIEM 2026 looks forward to your participation in moving towards a better future together.
We warmly invite you to participate in ICAIEM 2026 and look forward to seeing you!

Details of The Attending Registration
Join as Presenter
If you are only interested in giving a presentation at the conference without publishing your paper in the proceedings, you can choose to attend ICAIEM 2026 as a Presenter. As a presenter, you need to submit the Abstract and Title of your presentation during the registration. For further information, please contact us at: icaiem@126.com
Join as Listener
ICAIEM 2026 is an unmissable conference. It is a good chance and an effective platform for you to meet many renowned experts and researchers in the field of the latest academic research. You are welcome to attend this great event. You just need to complete the registration as a Listener before the registration deadline.
Details of The Registration Fees
1. Registration fees for ICAIEM2026 Regular is 540 USD / 3600 CNY per paper.
2. If the length of the paper exceeds 8 Pages, the cost of Each Extra Page (begin at Page 9) is 55 USD / 400 CNY.
3. Registration fees include conference proceedings, lunches, souvenirs and attending all technical sessions.
4. The registration fee does not include:
• Accommodation
• Visa application fee
• Transportation fare
5. At least one author for each accepted final paper must pre-register.
6. Completed registrations will be acknowledged by the Organizing Committee within 2-5 workdays after receiving your payment.
7.And at least 30% of the registration fee will be charged as the service fee if you retract your paper for personal reasons.
8. Important note to authors about the new ACM open access publishing model, all papers in the ICAIEM2026 proceedings will be published open access (OA) on the ACM Digital Library. For authors who are not affiliated with member institutions of the ACM Open Program, ACM charges an article processing charge (APC) for OA publication. However, the ICAIEM 2026 conference committee has agreed that the conference will pay all applicable APCs. All papers will therefore be published OA at no cost to authors.

Instruction for Oral Presentations
1. Timing: A maximum of 15 minutes in total, including presentation and discussion. Please be considerable once the Program is completed the next speaker should also be allocated with reasonable amount of time.
2. For conference presentation, you can use your own computer or copy your presentation file into the conference computer from CD or USB drive, and make sure you have scanned and cleaned viruses beforehand. Speakers are required to meet her/his session Chair in the corresponding session room at least 10 minutes before the session starts to get things ready or copy the slide file (PPT or PDF) into the conference computer.
3. It is also suggested that you email a copy of your presentation to your personal email box as a backup. If for some reason the files cannot be accessed from your flash drive, you can download them to the computer from your email.
4. Please note that each session room will be equipped with an LCD projector, screen, laser pointer, microphone, and a laptop with general presentation software such as the Microsoft PowerPoint and Adobe Reader. Please make sure that your files are compatible and readable with our operation system by using commonly used fronts and symbols. If you plan to use your own computer, please test the connection and make sure it works well before your presentation.
5. Movies: If your files contain movies please make sure that they are well formatted and connected to the main files.
Instructions for Poster Presentations
1. Maximum poster size is 59.4 CM wide by 84.1 CM high (A1).
2. Posters are required to be optimized and attractive. Information should be visible from a proper distance, say, one meter away.
3. Please note that during your poster session, you as the author should stay by your poster and ready to explain and discuss your paper with visiting delegates.

Link: History
Title: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2026)
2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025)
The event was successfully held at
Guangzhou College of Technology and Business.
June 28th, 2025，2025
International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Engineering Management (ICAIEM 2025）it was grandly held at the International Education Exchange Center of Guangzhou University of Commerce in San Shui District, Foshan.
This conference is jointly organized by Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and Guangdong Ais Information Academic Exchange Research Institute. It is supported by Guangzhou Dongruan University, Guangzhou Business College, Guangzhou City Polytechnic, Guangzhou Applied Science University, Guangzhou Xinhua University, Guangzhou Institute of Technology, and South China Agricultural University - Zhujiang College. It is jointly hosted by the School of
Management of Guangzhou Institute of Commerce and the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Smart Cold Chain Industry College.
The conference officially opened on the morning of June 28th at the Sansui Campus of Guangzhou Institute of Technology. The meeting was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from Guangzhou Institute of Technology. Representatives of the organizing committee warmly welcomed the presence of scholars from all walks of life. The conference was held in a combination of online and offline formats, gathering over 150 experts and scholars from around the world, including institutions such as Jinan University, Guangdong University of Technology, University of Sydney, Yunnan University, Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Liaoning Institute of Technology, Armed Police Engineering University, Southwest Forestry University, Jimei University, Guangdong Peizheng College, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., Zhuhai Science and Technology College Big Data College, and China Automobile Data Co., Ltd. The participants discussed in depth the latest research achievements and future development trends in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
Qianyuan Wang, the vice president of Guangzhou University of Commerce, delivered the opening speech on behalf of the organizers. He expressed his sincere gratitude to the domestic and foreign experts and scholars, as well as the sister universities and industry organizations, who have long been concerned and supported the development of the school. The conference focused on the hot and difficult issues in the field, promoting interdisciplinary research and the integration of industry-academia-research. It invited experts from multiple universities to have in-depth exchanges and jointly promote academic development and technological innovation. Vice President Wang hopes that all experts and scholars will offer valuable suggestions and work together to promote the innovative development of the engineering management discipline and contribute to the progress of the industry.
The meeting agenda consists of main venue reports, invited reports, and oral presentations. This main venue focuses on sharing on key topics such as "precision manufacturing around the value chain, post-5G industrial Internet of Things, digital consumer value co-creation, and green supply chain and corporate sustainable development". Professor Li Congdong from Jinan University, Professor Du Songhua from Guangdong University of Technology, Professor Yonghui Li from the University of Sydney in Australia, and Professor Zhiping Hou from Guangzhou University of Commerce respectively delivered keynote speeches. Experts and scholars presented their latest research results and academic insights in related fields, which aroused extensive attention and lively discussions among the attending experts and scholars. The academic atmosphere in the venue was very intense.
On the afternoon of the 28th, the afternoon session of the conference was chaired by Dr. Yujie Shi from the School of Management of Guangzhou Gongshang University. Associate Professor Hu Zuwu from Guangzhou Business College, Dr. Liu Chao from Guangzhou Gongshang University, Dr. Yujie Shi, and Dr.Yaqing Liang respectively delivered invited presentations. Subsequently, five experts from Guangzhou Business College, Jimei University, Hunan Economic Research and Power Design Co., Ltd., and Beijing Jiaotong University gave oral reports. The conference topics covered multiple cutting-edge development directions such as project risk assessment based on deep learning and large language models, image forgery detection, and the impact of supply chain digital transformation performance. It provided rich opportunities for academic exchanges and cooperation for the participants, promoting the continuous development and innovation of artificial intelligence and engineering management.
This event facilitated the exchange of scientific information among researchers, developers, engineers, students and practitioners in the fields of artificial intelligence and engineering management from all over the world. Through discussions and sharing, the practical experiences, thoughts and the most cutting-edge research achievements in related fields of scholars from around the globe were widely disseminated, which holds positive significance for addressing new challenges in the future.
